symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe holocephalic headache unrelieved by simple analgesia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A severe headache is part of the mediator-flare picture but is entirely non-specific.
Severe headache unresponsive to analgesia mandates active exclusion of CNS infection.
Any acute severe headache requires SAH to be actively excluded.
Severe throbbing headache is consistent with migraine.
Photophobia and phonophobia are core migraine features.
Photophobia is a meningism feature but non-discriminating on its own.
Sensory hypersensitivity is described in mediator-driven neuro flares.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute cognitive impairment / brain fog during the episode

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Transient neurocognitive slowing is reported in mast cell mediator flares.
New altered cognition with headache mandates exclusion of encephalitis / CNS infection.
Altered mental state can accompany intracranial haemorrhage.

symptoms.symptom_name: Facial and neck flushing preceding the headache

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Flushing preceding the headache is a characteristic mast cell mediator (histamine) feature.
Cutaneous flushing is not a feature of meningitis.
SAH classically peaks instantaneously (thunderclap); a gradual build lowers but does not exclude probability.
Migraine typically builds over minutes to hours rather than peaking instantly.

symptoms.symptom_name: No self-reported neck stiffness (able to flex neck to chest)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient-reported neck mobility is reassuring but self-report cannot replace formal meningeal-sign examination, so it does not exclude meningitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal neurological deficit reported (moving all limbs, normal speech)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a reported focal deficit lowers, but does not exclude, SAH.
No reported focal deficit is mildly reassuring but does not exclude encephalitis.
Absence of fever and rash lowers the probability of bacterial/meningococcal meningitis but does not exclude it, particularly early.

symptoms.symptom_name: Supple neck with negative Kernig and Brudzinski signs on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative meningeal signs on formal examination reduce meningitis probability — a sealed clinician finding not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact cranial nerves, symmetric power and no papilloedema on fundoscopy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal focused neurological examination and no papilloedema lower the probability of raised intracranial pressure / SAH — sealed clinician findings.
A normal neuro exam is reassuring but not exclusionary for CNS infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured vital signs stable and afebrile (BP normal, mild tachycardia, normal SpO2, temp normal)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile stable observations mildly lower the probability of sepsis/bacterial meningitis — clinician-measured and sealed.
Stable haemodynamics neither confirm nor exclude SAH.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known mast cell / histamine disorder with prior similar flares

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A background mast cell disorder with prior similar episodes raises the pre-test probability of a mediator flare — but must not be used to exclude the dangerous mimics.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Flushing preceding a severe headache with sensory hypersensitivity and cognitive fog, on a background of a known mast cell disorder, forms a coherent mediator-flare picture — but the same cluster cannot exclude meningitis/SAH remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line to exclude subarachnoid haemorrhage in an acute severe headache — arranged only via in-person / ED assessment; telehealth cannot order or interpret it.
Excludes meningitis/encephalitis and CSF xanthochromia for SAH if CT is negative — a hospital procedure, not achievable in telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum mast cell tryptase (drawn during the flare)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Supports mast cell mediator release retrospectively if drawn within a few hours of onset; does not change the need to exclude serious acute causes first.
Infection / inflammation screen as part of the in-person work-up.
